page: Amazon Web Services mac2.metal is a bare metal instance featuring the arm64_mac architecture. It is powered by an Apple processor running at 3.2 GHz, delivering 4 physical cores and 8 virtual CPUs with a dedicated allocation. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 2.0 GB of memory per core.

This instance does not include local storage, meaning storage resources must be attached externally. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration. The lack of integrated local storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local disk I/O or hardware-accelerated graphics processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the mac2.metal instance provides dedicated Apple silicon hardware within a cloud environment, offering a specialized resource density for macOS-specific tasks. This configuration is designed for workloads such as macOS and iOS application building, testing, signing, and continuous integration pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services mac2.metal is a bare metal instance built on the arm64_mac architecture, utilizing an Apple processor operating at 3.2 GHz. The system features 4 physical cores, 8 dedicated virtual CPUs, and 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or a dedicated GPU, requiring external storage solutions for data persistence. Network performance is supported by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Designed specifically for the macOS ecosystem, the mac2.metal instance offers a specialized hardware profile for developers. It is suited for workloads such as macOS and iOS application compilation, continuous integration and continuous delivery pipelines, and automated testing of Apple-targeted software.
og_description: Amazon Web Services mac2.metal is a bare metal macOS instance featuring an Apple arm64_mac processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of memory, and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
